Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 277 upvotes, 59 comments
  2. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 59 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. Cherri – programming language that compiles directly to a Apple Shortuct 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 1238 upvotes, 351 comments
  5. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 302 upvotes, 76 comments
  6. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 121 upvotes, 36 comments
  7. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 641 upvotes, 238 comments
  8. New Washington state law bans noncompete agreements 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 147 upvotes, 51 comments
  10. How to turn anything into a router 307 upvotes, 119 comments
  11. You are falling behind because you haven't fed the insincerity machine 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Comprehensive C++ Hashmap Benchmarks (2022) 46 upvotes, 13 comments
  13. VHDL's Crown Jewel 118 upvotes, 44 comments
  14. I use Excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 208 upvotes, 89 comments
  15. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 47 upvotes, 15 comments
  16. "Over 1.5 million GitHub PRs have had ads injected into them by Copilot" 292 upvotes, 141 comments
  17. Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed 77 upvotes, 34 comments
  18. FTC Action Against Match and OkCupid for Deceiving Users, Sharing Personal Data 85 upvotes, 41 comments
  19. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 892 upvotes, 579 comments
  20. Take Better Notes, by Hand 24 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. Parrots pack twice as many neurons as primate brains of the same mass 208 upvotes, 124 comments
  22. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 83 upvotes, 57 comments
  23. Bitwarden Integrates with OneCLI Agent Vault 38 upvotes, 23 comments
  24. From Proxmox to FreeBSD and Sylve in Our Office Lab 31 upvotes, 19 comments
  25. Spring Boot Done Right: Lessons from a 400-Module Codebase 71 upvotes, 60 comments
  26. 72% of the dollar's purchasing power was destroyed in just four episodes 164 upvotes, 157 comments
  27. In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? 47 upvotes, 37 comments
  28. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 293 upvotes, 325 comments
  29. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 110 upvotes, 110 comments
  30. OCR For construction documents does not work, we fixed it 16 upvotes, 10 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 31 March 2026. Source here.
